data.body:
PASS 1 review â€” T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: APPLICABLE. This plan defines LLM-evaluation artifacts (Inspect-AI eval specs with case schemas, deterministic + llm-judge scorers, an output-schema contract, and a labeled case-type / FP-mode vocabulary), so the overlay engages. I tool-verified every referenced structure; one PROVEN structural gap, the rest of the probe passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies â€” positive feedback, not findings):
- enum/value-vocabulary defined (no undefined-but-referenced enum): the FP taxonomy the plan defers to ("cover the whole taxonomy in that file") is fully enumerated and named â€” all 11 modes + the sycophancy axis are spelled out in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md:31-45, and the finder spec's `mode:` values (domain-inappropriate-standard-import, library-guaranteed-capability, rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference, low-impact-nitpick, not-applicable-library-or-cli) draw from that vocabulary (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:89-143), gated by test_finder_covers_observed_false_positive_modes (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:44-54). The output contracts referenced by scorers (`plan_review_findings`, `plan_review_verification`) are named, resolvable contracts.
- protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it: each spec co-locates its scoring protocol with its dataset â€” the finder's recall/false-fire scorers + the `expect: finding|pass` decision rule sit in the same file as the labeled cases (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:16-49); the verifier's discrimination rule ("planted-false validity STRICTLY LOWER") and the sycophancy floor (validity â‰Ą 0.5) are co-located with the {true,false} `pair`-linked cases (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:14-30, 49-94); ISF's recall + justified-descope-anti-FP scorers sit with the ISF cases (plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml:13-67).
- no ambiguous counter/state increment: the `epochs: 3` / `gate: at_least(2)` / `coverage_threshold: 0.8` accumulators are declared per-spec at a single site; the kappa-gold-subset mechanic is co-located (`gold_set` per spec).
- fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified: the offline-vs-live split is explicit and degrades safely â€” offline validation runs with no model/credentials (gated by tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py); the live model run is fenced behind the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + the eval CI (README.plan-review.md:14-24), and the T12 RECALL fixture the AC required is present + gated (R-T12-snap recall + T12-suppress-rebar suppression; test_finder_has_t12_discrimination_pair).
- no instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified above â€” all three eval specs, the taxonomy file, and the gating test exist and resolve).

FINDING (T8 â€” a case-type referenced in the value-vocabulary but never instantiated, with an under-discriminating gate):
- [ ] Pin which spec carries the "false-accept" (badâ†’pass / rubber-stamp) case type, or rename the third case type â€” the plan's AC#1 names "the three case types" = recall / false-accept / false-fire, and the finder spec header + README both advertise "false-accept (badâ†’pass)", but the finder dataset instantiates only recall (`expect: finding`) and false-fire (goodâ†’`expect: pass`) â€” there is NO labeled bad-planâ†’pass (false-accept) case in any spec's dataset; the inverse signal lives only as the verifier's `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` graded-validity scorer (an aggregate floor, not a labeled false-accept case). The gating test collapses the distinction (`expect in ("pass","low_validity")` buckets false-fire and false-accept together, tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:39-41), so the named three-value vocabulary is not actually enforced as three distinct types. State in the plan/AC where false-accept is realized (e.g. "false-accept = the verifier sycophancy axis, measured as graded validity â€” NOT a separate finder case") OR add a labeled badâ†’pass case so the third case type the AC promises is testable, not just named in a header comment.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: AC line 1 ("Per-criterion eval set with the three case types"); src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46 (header claims "recall / false-accept / false-fire"); README.plan-review.md:10 ("false-accept (badâ†’pass)"); tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:39-41 (gate collapses pass/low_validity)
  - evidence: AC#1 "the three case types"; finder spec comment "recall / false-accept / false-fire cases" (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2) but the dataset's only `expect: pass` cases (FP1/FP4/FP5/FP6/T12-suppress/FP7) are all GOOD plans (false-fire), not badâ†’pass; ABSENCE of any bad-plan-with-`expect: pass` case across all three specs; the inverse axis exists only as the verifier scorer `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:26-30) â€” this is the T8 "schema/enum value referenced but whose vocabulary is never (fully) defined/instantiated" antipattern (third value named, never realized as a discriminable case).
  - suggested_fix: (empty â€” the author should choose between binding "false-accept" to the verifier sycophancy axis in the AC/README, or adding an explicit badâ†’pass labeled case; both are defensible.)
